name: Italian Flat Bread (Focaccia)
recipeIngredient: 2 1/4 teaspoons Active dry yeast (or Quick-Rise yeast)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour, yeast, 1 tablespoon sugar, and 1 teaspoon salt. Mix well.
2. Add water and oil to the flour mixture. Blend at low speed until moistened.
3. Beat for 3 minutes at medium speed.
4. Gradually stir in enough remaining flour to make a firm dough. Knead on a floured surface for 5-8 minutes, adding flour as needed.
5. Place dough in a greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place about 40 minutes (20 minutes for Quick-Rise yeast).
6. Prepare onion topping: In a small skillet, sauté onions in butter until soft. Stir in 1/3 teaspoon sugar and 1/8 teaspoon salt.
7. Punch down dough. Shape dough into a ball on a lightly floured surface.
8. Place the ball on a greased cookie sheet. Flatten to a 10-inch circle.
9. With a table knife, cut a circle about 1 inch from the edge, cutting almost through to the cookie sheet.
10. Prick the center with a fork. Spread onion topping over the pricked dough.
11. Cover and let rise in a warm place about 30 minutes (15 minutes for Quick-Rise yeast).
12. Bake at 375°F for 25-30 minutes until golden brown.
Variation: Omit onion topping. Instead of pricking the top, cut a criss-cross design with a sharp knife. Brush bread with an egg glaze made by combining 1 egg yolk and 1 tablespoon milk or water. Sprinkle with sesame seeds if desired.
